title: Immunotherapy targeting core binding factor antigens
abstract: The present disclosure provides compositions and methods for targeting a Core Binding Factor, Î²-subunit:Myosin Heavy Chain 11 (CBFÎ²:MYH11) antigen to, for example, prevent or manage relapse of Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML). Also provided are polynucleotides and transgene constructs encoding binding proteins, such as a T cell receptor or a chimeric antigen receptor. Such transgene constructs can be transduced into an immune cell, such as a T cell, and used in immunotherapy in a subject having or at risk for recurrence of the AML.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An isolated polynucleotide encoding a binding protein that comprises a T cell receptor (TCR) a chain variable (Va) domain and a TCR b chain variable (nb) domain, wherein the encoded binding protein is capable of specifically binding to a peptide containing a Core Binding Factor subunit b Myosin Heavy chain 11 (EBRb:MUH11) antigen comprising or consisting of the amino acid sequence REEMEVHEL (SEQ ID NO:2) or EEMEVHEL (SEQ ID NO:3), wherein the polynucleotide is codon optimized for expression in a host cell. 2. The isolated polynucleotide of claim 1, wherein the EBRb:MUH11 antigen comprises or consists of the amino acid sequence REEMEVHEL (SEQ ID NO:2). 3. The isolated polynucleotide of claim 1 or 2, wherein the polynucleotide encoding the nb domain comprises: (i) a TCRBV05-0l*0l gene segment, a TCRBD02-0l*02 gene segment, and a TCRBJ02-02*0l gene segment; (ii) a TCRBV04-0l*0l gene segment, a TCRBD0l-0l*0l gene segment, and a TCRBJ02-0l*0l gene segment; (iii) a TCRBV04-0l*0l gene segment, a TCRBD02-0l*0l gene segment, and a T CRB JO 1-01*01 gene segment; (iv) a TCRBV07-02*0l gene segment, a TCRBDO 1-01*01 gene segment, and a TCRBJ02-0l*0l gene segment; (v) a TCRBV06-0l*0l gene segment, a TCRBDO 1-01*01 gene segment, and a TCRBJ02-0l*0l gene segment; or (vi) a TCRBV04-02*0l gene segment, a TCRBD gene segment, and a TCRBJ01-04*01 gene segment. 4. The isolated polynucleotide of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the polynucleotide encoding the Va domain comprises: (i) a TCRAV20-01 gene segment, a TCRDD01-01 gene segment, and a TCRAJ45-0l*0l gene segment; (ii) a TCRAV08-06 gene segment, a TCRAD gene segment, and a TCRAJ52-0l*0l gene segment; or (iii) a TCRAV38-01 gene segment, a TCRBDO 1-01*01 gene segment, and a TCRAJ50-0l*0l gene segment. 5. The isolated polynucleotide of claim 4, wherein: (i) the polynucleotide encoding the Va domain comprises a TCRAV20-01 gene segment, a TCRDD01-01 gene segment and a TCRAJ45-0l*0l gene segment, and the polynucleotide encoding the nb domain comprises a TCRBV07-02*0l gene segment, a TCRBD0l-0l*0l gene segment, and a TCRBJ02-0l*0l gene segment; (ii) the polynucleotide encoding the Va domain comprises a TCRAV08-06 gene segment, a TCRAD gene segment, and a TCRAJ52-0l*0l gene segment, and the polynucleotide encoding the nb domain comprises a TCRBV06-0l*0l gene segment, a TCRBDO 1-01*01 gene segment, and a TCRBJ02-0l*0l gene segment; (iii) the polynucleotide encoding the Va domain comprises a TCRAV38-01 gene segment, a TCRD gene segment, and a TCRAJ50-0l*0l gene segment, and the polynucleotide encoding the nb domain comprises a TCRBV04-0l*0l gene segment, a TCRBD02-0l*0l gene segment, and a TCRBJ01-01*01 gene segment; or (iv) the polynucleotide encoding the Va domain comprises a TCRAV38-01 gene segment, a TCRAD gene segment, and a TCRAJ50-0l*0l gene segment, and the polynucleotide encoding the nb domain comprises a TCRBV05-0l*0l gene segment, a TCRBD02-0l*02 gene segment, and a TCRBJ02-0l*02 gene segment. 6. The isolated polynucleotide of any one of claims 1-5, wherein: (i) the encoded nb domain comprises or consists of an amino acid sequence having at least 90% identity to the amino acid sequence set forth in any one of SEQ ID NOs: 17-20; and (ii) the encoded Va domain comprises or consists of an amino acid sequence having at least 90% identity to the amino acid sequence set forth in any one of SEQ ID NOs:2l-24. 7. The isolated polynucleotide of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the encoded nb domain comprises a Complementarity Determining Region 3 (CDR^) comprising or consisting of the amino acid sequence set forth in any one of SEQ ID NOs:4-8 or 12, or a variant thereof. 8. The isolated polynucleotide of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the encoded Va domain comprises a Complementarity Determining Region 3 (CDR3a) comprising or consisting of the amino acid sequence set forth in any one of SEQ ID NOs: 13-16, or a variant thereof. 9. The isolated polynucleotide any one of claims 6-8, wherein the encoded nb domain has at least 90% identity the amino acid sequence set forth in any one of SEQ ID NOs: 17-20, and wherein the encoded Va domain has at least 90% identity the amino acid sequence set forth in any one of SEQ ID NOs:2l-24, provided that: (a) at least three or four of the CDRs have no mutations; (b) the CDRs that do have mutations have only up to two amino acid substitutions, up to a contiguous five amino acid deletion, or a combination thereof; and (c) the encoded binding protein retains its ability to bind to a peptide comprising a OBEb:MUH11 antigen. 10. The isolated polynucleotide of claim 8 or 9, wherein the encoded CDR3b comprises or consists of the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO:6, and the 

 encoded CDR3a comprises or consists of the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: l5. 11. The isolated polynucleotide of claim 54 or 55, wherein the encoded self- cleaving peptide comprises or consists of the amino acid sequence set forth in any one of SEQ ID NOs:4l-44. 57. The isolated polynucleotide of claim 56, wherein the polynucleotide encoding the self-cleaving peptide comprises or consists of the nucleotide sequence set forth in any one of SEQ ID NOs:58-62. 58. The isolated polynucleotide of claim 59, wherein the polynucleotide encoding the selection marker comprises or consists of the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 63. 61. An isolated polynucleotide comprising a nucleotide sequence having at least 80% identity to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO:73. 62. The host cell, wherein the host cell is T cell, a NK cell, or a NK-T cell. 68. The host cell of claim 67, wherein the T cell comprises a CD8 + T, a CD4 + T cell, or both. 69. A modified immune cell, comprising a heterologous polynucleotide encoding a binding protein that includes: (a) a T cell receptor (TCR) a chain variable (Va) domain comprising a CDR3a comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in any one of SEQ ID NOs: 13-16, and a TCR b chain variable (nb) domain; (b) a TCR nb domain comprising a CDR3a comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in any one of SEQ ID NOs:4-8 or 12, and a TCR Va domain; or (c) a TCR Va domain of (a) and a TCR nb domain of (b), 

 wherein the encoded binding protein is expressed at the cell surface of the modified immune cell, and wherein the encoded binding protein is capable of specifically binding to a peptide containing an a Core Binding Factor subunit b Myosin Heavy chain 11 (OBEb:MUH11) antigen comprising or consisting of the amino acid sequence REEMEVHEL (SEQ ID NO:2) or EEMEVHEL (SEQ ID NO:3). 70. The modified immune cell of any one of claims 69-103, wherein the immune cell comprises a chromosomal gene knockout of a PD-l gene; a LAG3 gene; a TIM3 gene; a CTLA4 gene; an HLA component gene; a TCR component gene; or any combination thereof. 105. The modified immune cell of claim 104, wherein the chromosomal gene knockout comprises a knockout of an HLA component gene selected from an al macroglobulin gene; an a2 macroglobulin gene; an a3 macroglobulin gene; a bÎ microglobulin gene; or a b2 microglobulin gene; or any combination thereof. 106. The modified immune cell of claim 104 or 105, wherein the chromosomal gene knockout comprises a knockout of a TCR component gene selected from a TCR a variable region gene; a TCR b variable region gene; a TCR constant region gene; or any combination thereof. 107. The modified immune cell of any one of claims 69-106, further comprising a heterologous polynucleotide encoding: (i) a safety switch protein; (ii) a selection marker; (iii) a CD8 co-receptor b-chain; (iv) a CD8 co-receptor a-chain; or (v) any combination thereof. 108. The modified immune cell of claim 107, wherein the encoded selection marker comprises or consists of the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO:45, and wherein the polynucleotide encoding the selection marker optionally comprises or consists of the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO:63. 109. A composition, comprising a (i) a host cell according to any one of claims 66-68; or (ii) a modified immune cell of any one of claims 69-108, and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier, diluent, or excipient. 110. A unit dose, comprising a therapeutically effective amount of (i) a modified immune cell of any one of claims 69-108 or (ii) a composition of claim 109. 111. A method for treating or for preventing a relapse of Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML) in a subject, the method comprising administering to the subject a unit dose of claim 109, thereby treating or preventing a relapse of AML in the subject. 112. The method of claim 111, wherein the subject expresses a CBFpAIYH l 1 antigen comprising or consisting of the amino acid sequence REEMEVHEL (SEQ ID NO:2); or EEMEVHEL (SEQ ID NO:3). 113. The method of claim 111 or 112, wherein the subject is HLA-A*02:0l + ; HLA-A*03:0l + ; HLA-A*l l :0l + ; HLA-B*40:0l + ; HLA-B*40:03 + ; HLA-B*44:02 + ; or HLA-B*44:03 + . 114. The method of any one of claims 111-113, wherein the subject is receiving or previously received a hematopoietic cell transplant (HCT). 115. The method of claim 114, wherein the HCT comprises a donor hematopoieitic cell comprising a chromosomal knockout of a gene that encodes an HLA component, a chromosomal knockout of a gene that encodes a TCR component, or both. 116. The method of any one of claims 111-115, wherein the subject had previously received lymphodepleting chemotherapy. 117. The method of claim 116, wherein the lymphodepleting chemotherapy comprises cyclophosphamide, fludarabine, anti -thymocyte globulin, or a combination thereof. 118. The method of any one of claims 111-117, wherein one or more of the modified immune cells comprised in the unit dose is autologous to the subject. 119. The method of any one of claims 111-118, further comprising administering an inhibitor of an immune checkpoint molecule to the subject.
